description: Command frontmatter is missing the allowed-tools field; Claude Code cannot determine which tools to pre-authorise when dispatching the command.
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools to frontmatter listing tools the command invokes (e.g., Task, Skill, Read, Glob).

description: plugin.json version 5.1.0 does not match package.json version 1.0.0; the plugin's own enhance-plugins skill flags this as a HIGH-certainty structural bug.
suggested_fix: Align both version fields to the same semver string (e.g., bump package.json to 5.1.0).

description: Binary agent-analyzer is fetched from GitHub releases at runtime via HTTPS on first use. SHA-256 sidecar and optional SLSA attestation provide integrity checks, but a stolen release token plus matching SHA-256 sidecar would bypass the checksum gate.
suggested_fix: Document opt-in prominently; consider bundling or requiring explicit user consent before first download.

description: SLSA attestation is soft-fail by default: if the gh CLI is absent, verification is skipped with a warning. Only SHA-256 protection remains, which does not close the stolen-release-token attack vector.
suggested_fix: Document AGENT_ANALYZER_REQUIRE_ATTESTATION=1 in README and recommend enabling it in CI.

description: Agent has no examples block; zero examples in an agent definition reduces reliability for edge cases and reduces test coverage.
suggested_fix: Add an <examples> block with at least two input/output pairs showing the agent's analysis output.

description: Agent has no examples block.
suggested_fix: Add an <examples> block with at least two input/output pairs.

suggested_fix: Add a brief Output Format section describing the response structure the agent returns.

description: Description uses 'better AI understanding' — a vague outcome claim that does not specify what constitutes 'better'.
suggested_fix: Replace with measurable outcome, e.g., 'to improve instruction-following accuracy and reduce token waste'.

description: 'Structural analysis (no complex reasoning needed)' — 'complex' is vague and context-dependent.
suggested_fix: Replace with 'pattern matching without multi-step inference'.

description: 'Balance AI optimization with human readability' — 'balance' does not specify a measurable trade-off point.
suggested_fix: Specify the trade-off: e.g., 'prefer readability when token savings are below 15%'.

description: 'Be cautious about security patterns' — 'cautious' gives no measurable direction.
suggested_fix: Replace with 'Prefer false positives over false negatives for security findings; report all matches above LOW certainty'.

description: 'Consider skill context when evaluating trigger quality' — 'consider' is a weak directive.
suggested_fix: Replace with 'Always evaluate trigger quality in the context of the skill's declared invocation mode (user-invocable vs. auto-only)'.

description: The 8 Critical Rules list mandates without rationale; rules without WHY explanations have lower compliance rates.
suggested_fix: Add a one-line WHY comment after each rule, e.g., 'Plain text output — emojis break token-efficient parsing in downstream agents'.

description: Four vague terms in detection patterns: 'complex logic', 'simple and fast', 'unreasonably long timeouts', used without quantification — conflicts with the skill's own requirement for precise pattern matching.
suggested_fix: Quantify: 'logic exceeding 50 lines', 'execution time >500ms', 'timeout >90s'.

description: 'Limit to 3-5 relevant tools' uses 'relevant'; 'Consider dynamic tool loading for large toolsets' uses 'consider' and 'large' without thresholds.
suggested_fix: Replace 'relevant' with 'task-scoped'; define 'large' as '>15 tools'.

description: 'Consider skill context when evaluating trigger quality' and 'Missing argument-hint for skills that clearly need input' use 'consider' and 'clearly' without measurable criteria.
suggested_fix: Replace 'consider' with 'always'; define 'clearly need input' as 'skill body references $ARGUMENTS'.

description: 'Positioning suggestions are HIGH certainty but may have valid exceptions' — 'may have' is a hedge that reduces the prescriptive value of the guidance.
suggested_fix: List specific valid exceptions explicitly rather than hedging with 'may'.

description: plugin.json version 5.1.0 does not match package.json version 1.0.0; the plugin's own schema requires these to be in sync.
suggested_fix: Run the enhance:plugin command on this repo and apply the version-sync auto-fix.

description: All 8 enhancer agents advertise --fix apply behaviour but declare no Write or Edit tools; if skills cannot bypass agent tool restrictions, auto-fix is silently broken across all agents.
suggested_fix: Either add Write and Edit to each agent's tools list, or explicitly document that Skill invocations bypass agent-level tool restrictions.
